summary:
The primary destination for lymph from the middle of the lower lip is the submental nodes because it aligns with known regional anatomy and drainage pathways. This information supports option B as being accurate based on standard medical understanding. \boxed{Submental Nodes}
The main point here involves identifying where specific facial area lymph flows predominantly go according to established anatomic principles related to local node distribution during normal physiological conditions or when dealing with potential pathology affecting these regions. 

Key Points Summarized In Two Sentences:
- Primary lymphatic drainage from mid-lower lips goes mainly to submental nodes per recognized anatomical paths.
- Understanding this helps pinpoint likely affected nodal groups clinically if issues arise within that face region. 
\boxed{\text{Submental Nodes}} To be clear about what was summarized concisely while focusing only on critical aspects pertinent to answering "where does [this] drain?" type questions regarding head/neck structures' associated lymphatics behaviorally under typical circumstances without extraneous details not essential to core diagnostic/research queries at hand.
